Definition: Total number of simulated time steps
Notes: Daily in v0.1; can be hourly in later versions

Definition: Duration represented by one simulation step
Notes: Hourly resolution is recommended for lunch-peak experiments

Definition: Number of repeated stochastic simulation runs per scenario-policy combination
Role in Model: Supports confidence intervals and statistical tests
Notes: Increase when stochastic variability is large

Definition: Set of random seeds used for repeated experiments
Notes: Keep same seed set across compared models

Definition: Number of student agents or aggregate student population represented in the simulation
Calibration / Data Source: Campus enrollment or canteen user count
Notes: Can be segmented into student groups

Definition: Number of food vendors operating in the ecosystem
Role in Model: Determines production diversity and capacity distribution
Notes: Can change under new-vendor or vendor-exit scenarios

Definition: Number of supplier agents serving vendors
Notes: Multiple suppliers improve disruption modeling

Definition: Number of delivery agents or delivery capacity units
Calibration / Data Source: Delivery platform or campus logistics data
Notes: Can be zero for non-delivery baseline

Definition: Reference student meal demand before scenario multipliers
Calibration / Data Source: Historical meal sales or survey data
Notes: Used with scenario demand multiplier

Definition: Scenario-specific multiplier applied to base demand
Calibration / Data Source: Scenario design or historical shock data
Role in Model: Models demand surge or demand reduction
Notes: Exam week may use μ_D = 1.30–1.60

Definition: Random variation around expected demand
Notes: Set to zero for deterministic experiments

Definition: Distribution of student arrivals or meal requests over time
Notes: Recommended for future high-resolution simulation

Definition: Effect of meal price on student demand probability
Notes: Higher magnitude means stronger price sensitivity

Definition: Effect of nutrition score on student vendor or menu choice
Notes: Can change under nutrition campaign scenario

Definition: Maximum meals a vendor can produce per time step
Role in Model: Limits production and affects shortage risk
Notes: Can be reduced by vendor failure or equipment failure

Definition: Planned vendor capacity utilization
Notes: Can exceed 1.0 to represent overload in stress tests

Definition: Number of meals produced by vendor v at time t
Role in Model: Determines served meals, leftovers, and waste
Notes: May be constrained by resource availability

Definition: Efficiency with which vendor mission workload becomes useful meals
Notes: Can fall under disruption or labor shortage

Definition: Maximum ingredient supply or delivery capacity per time step
Notes: Can be reduced under supply-chain disruption

Definition: Scenario-specific multiplier applied to available supply
Role in Model: Models supply shock or supply expansion
Notes: Supply shock may use μ_S = 0.50–0.80

Definition: Ingredient/resource amount required per meal produced
Notes: Different menus may have different coefficients

Definition: Ingredient inventory available to vendor v
Notes: Not fully implemented in v0.1

Definition: Maximum ingredient inventory that vendor v can store
Notes: Important for perishable goods

Definition: Fraction of inventory that becomes unusable per time step
Notes: Relevant for sustainability analysis

Definition: Selling price per meal from vendor v
Notes: Can be constrained by university policy

Definition: Cost incurred by vendor v to produce one meal
Notes: Affected by inflation scenario

Definition: Scenario-specific multiplier applied to operating cost
Notes: Inflation may use μ_C = 1.20–1.50

Definition: Fixed operating cost per vendor per time step
Notes: Not fully implemented in v0.1

Definition: Subsidy provided by university per eligible meal
Notes: Can be activated under inflation or scholarship scenarios

Definition: Produced meals not served by vendor v
Notes: Some leftovers may be donated or reused in future versions

Definition: Fraction of leftover meals that becomes waste
Notes: Lower in MOEE due to better coordination

Definition: Total food waste generated by the ecosystem
Notes: Can be converted to kg or CO2 equivalent

Definition: Average nutrition quality of meals provided by vendor v
Notes: Can be affected by nutrition policy

Definition: Minimum or desired ecosystem-level nutrition score
Role in Model: Sets nutrition component of mission achievement
Notes: Can increase under nutrition policy scenario

Definition: Penalty applied to student satisfaction for waiting time
Role in Model: Translates queue delay into satisfaction loss
Notes: Important for hourly queue simulation

Definition: Weight of affordability in student satisfaction or EVI
Role in Model: Determines importance of price relative to other benefits
Notes: Weights should sum appropriately within utility function

Definition: Weight of convenience in student satisfaction
Role in Model: Determines effect of distance, delivery, and time saving
Notes: Can increase under extreme weather scenario

Definition: Probability that stakeholder i accepts an MSRS recommendation
Calibration / Data Source: Behavioral data or assumed trust model
Role in Model: Determines whether recommendations affect mission intensity
Notes: Depends on trust, benefit, cost, and autonomy

Definition: Trust of stakeholder i in the operator, PUDAL, or MSRS recommendation
Notes: Can change through PUDAL learning

Definition: Degree to which stakeholder i resists external recommendations
Notes: Important for realistic multi-agent behavior

Definition: Effect of expected benefit on recommendation acceptance
Role in Model: Converts expected gain into adoption probability
Notes: Part of stakeholder acceptance equation

Definition: Effect of implementation cost on recommendation acceptance
Role in Model: Converts effort or cost into adoption penalty
Notes: Important for vendor and supplier recommendations

Definition: Weights used to aggregate normalized metrics into EVI
Notes: Sensitivity analysis recommended

Definition: Rate at which PUDAL updates policy or model parameters after observing outcomes
Notes: v0.1 logs outcomes but does not yet perform adaptive learning

Definition: Difference between predicted and realized demand or performance
Notes: Used for AI forecasting upgrade scenario

Definition: Scenario-specific multiplier applied to available operating capacity
Role in Model: Models vendor failure, pandemic, or equipment failure
Notes: Vendor failure may use μ_cap = 0.60–0.80

Definition: Availability of the online ordering or coordination platform
Calibration / Data Source: Platform uptime logs or scenario design
Notes: Not fully implemented in v0.1

Definition: Intensity of regulatory inspection, constraint, or enforcement
Notes: Increases during food safety incident

Definition: Fraction of demand served by the ecosystem
Notes: A key component of EVI

Definition: Composite measure of how well ecosystem mission targets are achieved
Notes: Includes fulfillment, nutrition, satisfaction, and sustainability

Definition: Weighted composite index of ecosystem performance
Notes: Used for policy ranking and robustness analysis

Definition: Aggregate robustness score of policy k across scenarios
Notes: Used in Table 20
